hard.next_step: 5.22.26 KC: Alberto to know where budgets stand in early June. Follow up 2nd week in June. 4.1.26 KC: Follow up with lberto Kunstmann as he requested April 6 week he should know more about Procurement budget commitments. 2.13.26 KC: Engage with Alberto Kunstmann to determine interest level of exploring ANA/Intake etc.
hard.ais_why:
• Customer already runs two active pilots involving MS Copilot for internal solutions, signaling real commitment to AI-driven optimization.
• Stated needs include enhanced AI-driven optimization as an ongoing priority and embedding AI/automation in manual RFP, bidding, and evaluation workflows.
• Next step is to engage Alberto Kunstmann to gauge interest in ANA and Intake. Both modules explicitly named for follow-up.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal dark for roughly 250 days: no buyer contact since the 29 Oct 2025 discovery (the 13 Nov session was a buyer no-show), and three rep follow-ups across Feb, Apr, and May 2026 drew no reply. Stuck at Qualified with no demo ever delivered. Revive Alberto and Sandra now or disqualify.
north_star_verdict.math: Created and qualified 29 Oct 2025. Last real buyer engagement 29 Oct 2025 discovery (~250 days ago); the 13 Nov 2025 call was internal-only, buyer did not join. Close date 25 Sep 2026 (81 days out) after a 12 Jan 2026 push from 27 Mar 2026. Stage has never moved past Qualified in ~250 days. Zero field-history movement on Stage, Amount, or Forecast since creation. Time dark exceeds any credible Qualified cadence; the promised solution-consultant demo was never held.
evidence: LastActivityDate 13 Nov 2025; that session was a buyer no-show (Kevin: 'looks like they're not gonna join, we're 6 minutes in'), so last true buyer engagement is the 29 Oct 2025 discovery
Three rep follow-up attempts logged in Next_Step__c (13 Feb, 1 Apr, 22 May 2026) with no buyer reply
StageName stuck at Qualified since 29 Oct 2025 with no progression to Formal Evaluation
The 30-minute solution-consultant session and 45-minute demo Kevin promised on 29 Oct 2025 were never delivered
CloseDate pushed from 27 Mar 2026 to 25 Sep 2026 on 12 Jan 2026 with no accompanying activity
Forecast Pipeline is indefensible on a dormant, no-champion deal; recommend Omitted until the buyer re-engages

metrics.narrative: Alberto Kunstmann named real operational pain on 29 Oct 2025 but never quantified it: their RFP/bidding cycle in Sabariva is 'very manual today' across invite, quotation, comparative box and evaluation, and supplier relationship, onboarding, and risk mapping are gaps he said 'we need to develop.' He gave no cost per RFP cycle, no headcount on manual bid analysis, no cycle-time baseline, and no savings target, so there is no value case to defend a $200k spend. Quantifying the manual-process cost with Alberto and finance is the move before any commercial step.

economic_buyer.narrative: Sandra Montiel, Vice President Supply Chain, is the Salesforce Decision Maker and Alberto's boss (Alberto: 'I report to the supply chain vice president'), which makes her the economic authority for this initiative, but she has never appeared on a call, task, or follow-up. Budget ownership sits in procurement: Kevin's Next_Step notes have chased Alberto since Feb 2026 to learn 'where budgets stand' and confirm 'Procurement budget commitments,' evidence that the money is not yet allocated. The EB is mapped by title but completely unengaged; getting a Sandra-level conversation and a confirmed budget line is the gating move.
sources: OpportunityContactRole: Sandra Montiel = Decision Maker, VP Supply Chain

decision_criteria.narrative: Alberto stated two priority evaluation areas on 29 Oct 2025: supplier management ('supplier relationship, development, onboarding, supplier risk map') and putting agentic AI into the manual RFP/bidding/transactional process, plus Spanish-language solution and support ('the solution could be in Spanish with Spanish support' — 'Yes, absolutely, has to be'). Sustainability/ESG surfaced as a corroborating interest Kevin raised from their public materials. No formal ShoeFit, BRD, RFP round, or use-case list exists yet, so the criteria are directional not confirmed; the promised scoping session was meant to refine exactly what they want to see.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer's decision process is essentially unmapped. The call sequence shows only Alberto Kunstmann plus Gustavo (business improvement) on the 29 Oct 2025 discovery, then a canceled/no-show 13 Nov session and silence, so we have never seen who else validates, who signs, or how procurement runs. Alberto proposed 'a second meeting to review in more details' and specific dates, but that next step was never executed. The only visible gating step is internal budget confirmation Kevin has chased since Feb 2026; recovering a live call and asking Alberto to map the evaluation and approval steps is what would create visibility.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are known: no MSA/SOW discussion, no procurement channel, no legal or InfoSec contact, and no contract-expiration forcing function surfaced. This is a Chilean mining/utilities account on JD Edwards with a Chilean contract tool (Supply Net 'Portal Contratos'), so an eventual purchase would likely route through local procurement, but nothing has been confirmed. As new business in a new market, expect MSA plus Order Form plus SOW once commercials begin; today the deal is far upstream of paper, and paper visibility only starts after a demo and a business case land.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is confirmed and articulated by the pain owner, Alberto Kunstmann, on 29 Oct 2025: (1) supplier management is underbuilt ('what we miss, maybe it's something related to suppliers... supplier relationship, development, onboarding, supplier risk map... it is a thing that we need to develop'), and (2) the RFP-to-award cycle in Sabariva is 'very manual today' across inviting suppliers, receiving quotations, building the comparative box, and evaluating, and he is 'open to investigate new features' to replace it. It is real and owner-attributed, just not quantified, and it maps directly to iSupplier, iSource, iRisk, and Merlin/Agentic AI. The risk is that this pain has sat unaddressed for ~250 days while the buyer keeps managing manually.

champion.narrative: Alberto Kunstmann (Supply Chain Excellence Superintendent, primary contact, Evaluator) was the sole engaged buyer and showed early openness on 29 Oct 2025, articulating pain and asking for a second meeting with dates. But he is not a power buyer, has shown no advocacy or internal introductions, has never connected us to Sandra Montiel, and has gone silent for ~250 days across three follow-ups. He is best read as a cooling evaluator, not a champion: what developed is a clear pain and interest; what is missing is any evidence he is pushing this internally or has budget authority. The deal has no confirmed champion, and building one (Alberto re-activated, or Sandra directly) is the top continuous priority.

competition.narrative: The strongest current threat is do-nothing/status quo: after ~250 days of silence the buyer is simply continuing to manage RFPs manually in Sabariva and build lightweight automation themselves. The internal-build path on Microsoft Power Platform with Copilot pilots is a live 'buy vs build' alternative Alberto called 'more flexible and cheaper in my opinion,' reinforced by their heavy Microsoft investment and planned Teams migration. Sabariva is the incumbent RFP/sourcing tool being displaced, acknowledged as manual and inadequate. Alberto recognized Coupa, Jaggaer (Jager), and SAP Ariba as market peers when Kevin positioned Zycus, but gave no sign of an active evaluation of any of them. The wedge is Merlin agentic AI for bid analysis and supplier onboarding/risk that Power Platform and Sabariva cannot deliver at enterprise scale, plus embedding into Teams so we protect their Microsoft investment; that wedge remains theoretical because no demo has been shown.
sources: Avoma 29 Oct 2025, Kevin Cipollaro: 'Zycus is like a competitor of Sabariva, Coupa, Jager'

deal_movement.summary: No real forward movement since creation. The only substantive buyer touch is the 29 Oct 2025 discovery; the 13 Nov 2025 follow-up was a buyer no-show, and every activity since is rep-initiated chasing with no buyer reply. The single field-history event of note is a 6-month close-date push (12 Jan 2026) unaccompanied by any buyer activity.

items.change: Discovery call held with Alberto Kunstmann and Gustavo; pain and two priority areas established; second meeting agreed
Follow-up session canceled/buyer no-show (Kevin: 'looks like they're not gonna join, we're 6 minutes in')
CloseDate pushed 27 Mar 2026 to 25 Sep 2026 with no buyer activity
Rep follow-up logged to gauge ANA/Intake interest; no buyer reply
Rep follow-up logged awaiting Alberto on procurement budget commitments; no buyer reply
Rep follow-up logged awaiting budget standing in early June; no buyer reply

competitive_position.summary: Do-nothing / status quo is the single strongest current threat: ~250 days of silence means the buyer keeps running RFPs manually in Sabariva and building on Microsoft Power Platform themselves. Microsoft Power Platform + Copilot is the live buy-vs-build alternative (Alberto: 'more flexible and cheaper'). Sabariva is the incumbent RFP/sourcing tool to displace, acknowledged as manual. Coupa, Jaggaer, and SAP Ariba were named only as market peers on 29 Oct 2025, with no evidence of an active evaluation. All competitive intel is from 29 Oct 2025; nothing new this run.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / Status Quo
competitors.quote: ~250 days of buyer silence since 29 Oct 2025 with no response to three rep follow-ups; deal dormant at Qualified
competitors.how_we_win: Re-engage Alberto and multi-thread to Sandra Montiel with a quantified manual-process cost and Merlin ROI tied to the FY budget cycle to create urgency against inertia

competitors.name: Microsoft Power Platform + Copilot (internal build)
competitors.quote: We are implementing Microsoft Power Platform... more flexible and cheaper in my opinion... we also have to pilot with Copilot, two solutions we are developing
competitors.how_we_win: Concede Power Platform for lightweight internal workflows; position Merlin agentic bid-analysis, supplier onboarding and risk scoring as the enterprise supplier-facing layer it cannot build, and embed into Teams to protect their Microsoft investment

competitors.name: Sabariva
competitors.quote: Today use Sabariva to do our business and negotiation and all the stage of the RFP... it's very manual today, and we are open to investigate new features to replace what we have today
competitors.how_we_win: Displace on agentic automation of the manual quotation-comparison and evaluation cycle Alberto described, quantifying cycle-time and cost reduction vs Sabariva's manual workflow

competitors.name: Coupa / Jaggaer / SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Zycus, it's like a competitor of Sabariva, Coupa, Jager... Yes
competitors.source: Avoma discovery 29 Oct 2025 (Kevin naming peers; buyer acknowledged, no active evaluation)
competitors.how_we_win: No active bake-off; keep the frame on Zycus internal-development architecture (extensible, faster/cheaper integrations) vs acquisition-built suites if any of these surface later

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Two clearly stated buyer expectations from 29 Oct 2025 map well to the Zycus scope (iSupplier, iSource, Merlin/Agentic AI), with Spanish-language delivery a hard requirement. Fit is promising on paper but untested: no demo has validated it.

items.criterion: Supplier relationship management: onboarding, development, risk mapping
items.quote: what we miss, maybe it's something related to suppliers... supplier relationship, development, onboarding, supplier risk map... it is a thing that we need to develop

items.criterion: Agentic AI to automate the manual RFP / bidding / evaluation cycle
items.quote: the other desire is related to... put agentics inside this manual process of the RFP or the transactional manual work related to bidding process
the solution could be in Spanish with Spanish support? Yes, absolutely, has to be

items.criterion: Protect Microsoft investment / integrate with Teams
items.quote: we are going to use Teams... we are going to integrate to Teams

items.deliverable: Alberto to confirm procurement budget standing and commitment for the initiative
items.grounding_quote: Alberto to know where budgets stand in early June... he should know more about Procurement budget commitments

items.quote: No buyer reply to three follow-ups (13 Feb, 1 Apr, 22 May 2026) since the 29 Oct 2025 discovery
Sandra Montiel (VP Supply Chain, Decision Maker) has never joined a call or activity
Pain articulated but never quantified (no cost, cycle-time, or headcount baseline)
Promised solution-consultant session and demo never held

best_practice_check.summary: This is a dark, single-threaded, early-stage deal with a strong pain-to-product fit that has never been demonstrated. The win path is to restart the relationship, get to the economic buyer, and finally deliver the demo that was promised.
flags: Re-open the relationship: the account owner should re-engage Alberto directly with a specific value hook (agentic bid analysis for their manual RFP cycle), not another generic budget-check ping.
Break single-threading now: Sandra Montiel is the VP/Decision Maker and has never been touched; a second thread to her is the highest-leverage way to test whether this initiative is real.

Convert fit to proof: the promised solution-consultant scoping session plus a Spanish-language demo on supplier management and agentic RFP is the fastest way to move off Qualified and beat the internal Power Platform build.

items.name: Sandra Montiel
items.title: Vice President Supply Chain
items.risk: Highest stakeholder risk: the economic authority (and Alberto's boss) has never participated in any call, task, or follow-up; without her the initiative has no confirmed sponsor or budget.

items.name: Alberto Kunstmann
items.title: Supply Chain Excellence Superintendent
items.risk: Sole engaged contact and not a power buyer; went dark after the discovery and never scheduled the demo he asked for. No advocacy or EB access shown.
items.sentiment: Cooling: initially open and requested a second meeting, then silent ~250 days across three follow-ups

champion_strength.summary: No confirmed champion. Alberto Kunstmann was the sole engaged contact and showed early interest on 29 Oct 2025 but has demonstrated no internal advocacy, no access to Sandra Montiel, and has been silent ~250 days across three follow-ups. He is a cooling evaluator, not a champion; the deal needs either Alberto re-activated with a clear stake or a direct line to Sandra.
champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole (Evaluator); Avoma discovery 29 Oct 2025; Next_Step__c 13 Feb / 1 Apr / 22 May 2026 (unanswered)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Strong latent AI appetite (two live Copilot pilots, explicit desire to embed agentic AI in RFP/bidding) but the Merlin/Agentic AI story has never been shown in a demo, so positioning is under-activated. The agentic bid-analysis and supplier-risk narrative is the wedge and has not yet been put in front of the buyer.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 7.0 / Moderate is corroborated by the calls: Alberto runs two MS Copilot pilots and explicitly wants agentic AI inside the manual RFP/bidding process and supplier risk mapping. Appetite is real but self-serve/internal-build oriented, so the Merlin enterprise-agentic differentiation must be demonstrated to convert curiosity into preference.

items.detail: Economic buyer Sandra Montiel (VP Supply Chain) has never engaged; the deal is single-threaded on a cooling evaluator.
Internal Microsoft Power Platform + Copilot build is seen as cheaper/more flexible; buy-vs-build inertia is the main threat.
Procurement budget commitment unconfirmed since Feb 2026 despite repeated rep chasing; no allocated funds evident.
No progression past Qualified in ~250 days and a 6-month close push; 25 Sep 2026 date is not credible without imminent re-engagement.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement has gone dark. The last genuine two-way exchange was the 29 Oct 2025 discovery; everything since is one-way rep outreach with no buyer response, and the promised next meeting never happened.
items: Buyer no-show on the 13 Nov 2025 follow-up
Three unanswered rep follow-ups (13 Feb, 1 Apr, 22 May 2026)
No stage movement and no demo in ~250 days

items.expected_effect: Restarts the relationship with value, not another budget ping; tests whether the initiative is still live

items.trigger: VP Supply Chain and Decision Maker has never engaged; deal is single-threaded on a cooling evaluator
items.expected_effect: Reaches the economic authority and surfaces whether budget and sponsorship exist, breaking the do-nothing risk

items.expected_effect: Creates the business case needed to secure budget and a Sandra-level sponsor conversation

customer_preference.evidence: Alberto was open on 29 Oct 2025 ('open to investigate new features') and asked for a second meeting, but has since gone silent ~250 days with no Zycus-specific next step pursued; no active preference signal.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only: manual RFP/bidding cycle and underbuilt supplier management; no cost, cycle-time, or savings figures quantified.

scope_change.from: Source-to-pay scope (iSource, iSupplier, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA) at $200k
scope_change.detail: Amount was set to $200k at creation (up from a $100k initial value the same day) and products have not narrowed; no scope reduction observed.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A well-qualified $200k source-to-pay deal with strong AI pain-fit that has been dark for ~250 days: only one real discovery call ever happened, the economic buyer has never engaged, and three rep follow-ups went unanswered, so the whole picture hinges on whether we can re-open the relationship.

win_position.text: This is an early-stage, single-threaded deal that has gone cold: the last genuine buyer exchange was the 29 Oct 2025 discovery and nothing has advanced past Qualified since.

The pain-to-product fit is genuinely strong: Alberto explicitly wants agentic AI in his manual RFP/bidding cycle and supplier onboarding/risk, matching the iSource, iSupplier, iRisk and Merlin scope (Avoma, 29 Oct 2025).
Economic buyer Sandra Montiel (VP Supply Chain) has never been touched and procurement budget has stayed unconfirmed since Feb 2026, so there is no sponsor and no proven funding.

The internal Microsoft Power Platform + Copilot build (Alberto: 'more flexible and cheaper') plus do-nothing inertia can win by default unless we get in front of them with a demo we have never delivered.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is effectively zero: the 13 Nov 2025 follow-up was a buyer no-show and three rep pings (Feb, Apr, May 2026) drew no reply.
The close date was quietly pushed six months (27 Mar to 25 Sep 2026) with no buyer activity behind it, so the date is a placeholder, not a plan.

customer_commitment.text: No commitment beyond early interest: Alberto asked for a second meeting on 29 Oct 2025 but never scheduled it and has not responded to any follow-up since.
The Decision Maker has never engaged and budget is unconfirmed, so the buyer has invested nothing that signals a real evaluation.

deal_risk.text: The dominant risk is no-decision/do-nothing: after ~250 days of silence the buyer keeps managing RFPs manually in Sabariva and building on Power Platform themselves.
Single-threading on a cooling evaluator with an unengaged VP economic buyer means one unanswered inbox has stalled the entire deal.

customer_engagement.evidence: Dark ~250 days; buyer no-show 13 Nov 2025; three unanswered follow-ups Feb-May 2026

economic_buyer.evidence: VP Supply Chain Sandra Montiel mapped but never engaged; budget unconfirmed since Feb 2026

champion.evidence: Alberto is a cooling evaluator with no advocacy or EB access

product_fit.evidence: Explicit agentic-AI and supplier-management pain maps cleanly to the Zycus scope (Avoma 29 Oct 2025)

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 2 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 10 is carried by momentum 55 vs stage-expected 50; exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 55 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 2 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (10), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 55, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
